title: Xtra Roasted Filipino Cheese Casserole
description: A kid-friendly Filipino dish featuring muscovado sugar, this casserole is roasted to perfection in a ttukbaegi earthenware cookware, making it a great option for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the cooked macaroni, muscovado sugar, and melted butter.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, salt, and black pepper.
Add the shredded edam cheese to the egg mixture and stir until well combined.
Add the cheese mixture to the macaroni mixture and stir until the macaroni is well coated.
Transfer the mixture to a ttukbaegi earthenware cookware or a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 35 minutes or until the top is golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
